reference_note: Reference has no single status field -- workflow is a chain of separate Submittable doctypes (Job Card Creation -> Water Wash Done -> Bay In/Out Creation -> Outwork Entry Issue/Return -> Final Inspection -> Service Invoice), documented in PRODUCTION_DELIVERY_REPORT.md:151 as Pending -> Water Wash -> In Bay -> In Progress -> Outwork (if needed) -> Final Inspection -> Ready -> Invoiced
evidence: service/models.py:92-101 (enum), 767-1173 (WaterWashDone/BayInCreation/BayOutCreation/OutworkEntryIssue/OutworkEntryReturn/FinalInspection stage models + post_save signals at 1139-1173 that advance service_status on submit) -- correctly reimplements the reference chain-of-doctypes as one status field advanced by signals

reference_note: (implicit in reference: each stage doctype naturally follows the prior one in the UI flow)
evidence: service/models.py:767-770 (_advance_job_card_status unconditionally overwrites service_status whenever it differs, no check against current value) + service/views.py:1033-1043 (bay_in_create), 1070-1080 (bay_out_create) etc. have no precondition on the JobCard current service_status -- a stage document (e.g. Bay Out Creation) can be created and submitted before its predecessor (Bay In Creation) exists, letting a Job Card skip stages forward, or regress backward to an earlier status if an earlier-stage doc is submitted after a later one already advanced it

field: manual status override bypasses stage pipeline
reference_note: (reference has no manual status field to override -- status is always the byproduct of which stage doctype was last submitted)
evidence: service/views.py:362-385 -- jobcard_status_update lets any user with service/edit permission and ownership of the Job Card POST an arbitrary service_status value (any pipeline stage, forward or backward) with no stage document created or submitted at all; the only guard is that status is locked once a Service Invoice exists (line 375). This is a UI-exposed escape hatch (used from jobcard_detail.html) that bypasses the entire stage-document audit trail the reference workflow relies on

field: Service Invoice requires Job Card at Ready
reference_note: PRODUCTION_DELIVERY_REPORT.md:156 -- Cashier workflow is "Search a completed job card -> review labor + spares + outwork + GST -> ... -> generate invoice", implying only a completed (Ready) job card should be invoiced
evidence: service/views.py (service_invoice_create) now rejects invoice creation unless job_card.service_status == JobCard.ServiceStatus.READY, redirecting with an error message; previously had zero status check at all, so a brand-new Job Card at Pending could be invoiced immediately, skipping the entire workshop pipeline. Fixed this session with TDD -- see service/tests.py::ServiceInvoiceCreateStatusGateTests, commit 6e8278d
